info.title: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM - Questions
info.description:
Last Updated January 2026

You can use Oracle REST APIs to view and manage data stored in Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM.

Whether you're experienced with or new to REST, use this guide to find what you need, including:

  • A quick start how-to that walks you through a simple request example.
  • Use cases that provide real-world solutions with code examples.
  • Detailed descriptions for each REST resource.
info.x-summary: Last Updated January 2026 You can use Oracle REST APIs to view and manage data stored in Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM.

parameters.name: questionsUniqID
parameters.description: This is the hash key of the attributes which make up the composite key for the Questions resource and used to uniquely identify an instance of Questions. The client should not generate the hash key value. Instead, the client should query on the Questions collection resource in order to navigate to a specific instance of Questions to get the hash key.

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: When this parameter is provided, the specified children are included in the resource payload (instead of just a link). The value of this query parameter is "all" or "". More than one child can be specified using comma as a separator. Example: ?expand=Employees,Localizations. Nested children can also be provided following the format "Child.NestedChild" (Example: ?expand=Employees.Managers). If a nested child is provided (Example: Employees.Managers), the missing children will be processed implicitly. For example, "?expand=Employees.Managers" is the same as "?expand=Employees,Employees.Managers" (which will expand Employees and Managers).

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description:
This parameter filters the resource fields. Only the specified fields are returned, which means that if no fields are specified, no fields are returned (useful to get only the links). If an indirect child resource is provided (Example: Employees.Managers), the missing children will be processed implicitly. For example, "?fields=Employees.Managers:Empname" is the same as "?fields=;Employees:;Employees.Managers:Empname" (which will only return the "Empname" field for Managers). the value of this query parameter is a list of resource fields. The attribute can be a direct (Example: Employees) or indirect (Example: Employees.Managers) child. It cannot be combined with expand query parameter. If both are provided, only fields will be considered.

Format: ?fields=Attribute1,Attribute2

Format for fields in child resource: ?fields=Accessor1:Attribute1,Attribute2

parameters.name: onlyData
parameters.description: The resource item payload will be filtered in order to contain only data (no links section, for example).

parameters.name: dependency
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies dependencies which are fields that are set before and rolled back after generating the response. Generally they are used to preview the effects of an attribute change. The fields specified in this parameter are always set in the resource instance in question. When a child resource collection is requested and the parameter is set, the fields will be set in the parent resource instance before generating the resource collection payload. The value of this query parameter is a set of dependency fields. Example: dependency=ProductId=2

Format: <attr1>=<val1>,<attr2>=<value2>

parameters.name: links
parameters.description:
This parameter can be used to show only certain links while accessing a singular resource or a resource collection. The parameter value format is a comma-separated list of : <link_relation>

Example:
self,canonical

parameters.name: REST-Framework-Version
parameters.description: The protocol version between a REST client and service. If the client does not specify this header in the request the server will pick a default version for the API.

parameters.name: Metadata-Context
parameters.description: If the REST API supports runtime customizations, the shape of the service may change during runtime. The REST client may isolate itself from these changes or choose to interact with the latest version of the API by specifying this header. For example: Metadata-Context:sandbox="TrackEmployeeFeature".

parameters.name: Effective-Of
parameters.description: This header accepts a string value. The string is a semi-colon separated list of <parameter-name>=<parameter-value>. It is used to perform effective date range operations. The accepted parameters are RangeMode, RangeSpan, RangeStartDate, RangeEndDate, RangeStartSequence and RangeEndSequence. The parameter values are always strings. The possible values for RangeMode are SET_LOGICAL_START, SET_LOGICAL_END, END_DATE, SET_EFFECTIVE_START, SET_EFFECTIVE_END, REPLACE_CORRECTION, REPLACE_UPDATE, RECONCILE_CORRECTION, CORRECTION, RECONCILE_UPDATE, UPDATE, ZAP and DELETE_CHANGES. The possible values for RangeSpan are PHYSICAL_ROW_END_DATE and LOGICAL_ROW_END_DATE. The values for RangeStartDate and RangeEndDate have to be a string representation of a date in yyyy-MM-dd format. The value for RangeStartSequence and RangeEndSequence must be strings such that when parsed they yield positive integers.

default.description: The following table describes the default response for this task.

CtrlQuestionLovVO.description:
The following properties are defined on the LOV link relation:
  • Source Attribute: ControllingQuestionCode; Target Attribute: QuestionCode
  • Display Attribute: QuestionCode

attachments.description: The attachments resource is a child of the questions resource. It includes details of the attachments in a question.

The following properties are defined on the LOV link relation:
  • Source Attribute: ClassificationCode; Target Attribute: LookupCode
  • Display Attribute: LookupCode

  • Source Attribute: ControllingAnswerCode; Target Attribute: AnswerCode
  • Display Attribute: AnswerCode

organizationContexts.description: The organizationContexts resource is a child of the questions resource. It provides the recruiting organization that the question applies to. This context is used only by the recruiting subscriber.

RatingModelVO.description:
The following properties are defined on the LOV link relation:
  • Source Attribute: RatingModelCode; Target Attribute: RatingModelCode
  • Display Attribute: RatingModelCode

answers.description: The answers resource is a child of the questions resource. It provides a list of choices that a user can select from when answering single or multiple choice questions.

jobFamilyContexts.description: The jobFamilyContexts resource is a child of the questions resource. It provides the job family that the question applies to. This context is used only by the recruiting subscriber.

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: When this parameter is provided, the specified children are included in the resource payload (instead of just a link). The value of this query parameter is "all" or "". More than one child can be specified using comma as a separator. Example: ?expand=Employees,Localizations. Nested children can also be provided following the format "Child.NestedChild" (Example: ?expand=Employees.Managers). If a nested child is provided (Example: Employees.Managers), the missing children will be processed implicitly. For example, "?expand=Employees.Managers" is the same as "?expand=Employees,Employees.Managers" (which will expand Employees and Managers).

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description:
This parameter filters the resource fields. Only the specified fields are returned, which means that if no fields are specified, no fields are returned (useful to get only the links). If an indirect child resource is provided (Example: Employees.Managers), the missing children will be processed implicitly. For example, "?fields=Employees.Managers:Empname" is the same as "?fields=;Employees:;Employees.Managers:Empname" (which will only return the "Empname" field for Managers). the value of this query parameter is a list of resource fields. The attribute can be a direct (Example: Employees) or indirect (Example: Employees.Managers) child. It cannot be combined with expand query parameter. If both are provided, only fields will be considered.

Format: ?fields=Attribute1,Attribute2

Format for fields in child resource: ?fields=Accessor1:Attribute1,Attribute2

parameters.name: onlyData
parameters.description: The resource item payload will be filtered in order to contain only data (no links section, for example).

parameters.name: links
parameters.description:
This parameter can be used to show only certain links while accessing a singular resource or a resource collection. The parameter value format is a comma-separated list of : <link_relation>

Example:
self,canonical

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: This parameter restricts the number of resources returned inside the resource collection. If the limit exceeds the resource count then the framework will only return the available resources.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Used to define the starting position of the resource collection. If offset exceeds the resource count then no resources are returned. Default value is 0.

parameters.name: totalResults
parameters.description: The resource collection representation will include the "estimated row count" when "?totalResults=true", otherwise the count is not included.  The default value is "false".

parameters.name: q
parameters.description:
This query parameter defines the where clause. The resource collection will be queried using the provided expressions. The value of this query parameter is one or more expressions. Example: ?q=Deptno>=10 and <= 30;Loc!=NY

Format: ?q=expression1;expression2

You can use these queryable attributes to filter this collection resource using the q query parameter:
  • AllJobFamiliesFlag; boolean; Indicates whether the question is applicable to all job families. This flag is only used for the questions defined for the recruiting subscriber.
  • AllJobFunctionsFlag; boolean; Indicates whether the question is applicable to all job functions. This flag is only used for the questions defined for the recruiting subscriber.
  • AllLocationsFlag; boolean; Indicates whether the question is applicable to all locations. This flag is only used for the questions defined for the recruiting subscriber.
  • AllOrganizationsFlag; boolean; Indicates whether the question is applicable to all organizations. This flag is only used for the questions defined for the recruiting subscriber.
  • AllowAdditionalCommentsFlag; boolean; Indicates whether a participant can provide additional comments while responding to a question. Valid values are Y and N. The default value is N.
  • AllowAttachmentsFlag; boolean; Indicates whether an attachment is allowed as a response to the question.
  • CandidateCode; string; Code identifying the candidate type to whom the question applies to. Only the recruiting subscriber uses this question.
  • CandidateCodeMeaning; string; Meaning of the candidate code. Valid values are Internal, External and Both.
  • ClassificationCode; string; Code classifying the use of the question defined for Recruiting subscribers, like prescreening, and disqualification. Valid values are in the common lookups list of values.
  • ClassificationMeaning; string; Meaning of the classification code.
  • ConditionalDisplayFlag; boolean; Indicates whether a question is conditionally displayed. When this is set to Y, controlling question code and controlling response are mandatory.
  • CreatedBy; string; Name of the user who created the question.
  • CreationDate; string; Date when the question was created.
  • Editor; string; Person identifier of the person with the privileges to edit the question, if the question is marked as private. Required if question is marked as private.
  • EditorPersonName; string; Name of the person with privileges to edit the question marked as private. Required if question is marked as private.
  • EditorPersonNumber; string; Person number of the person with privileges to edit the question marked private. Required if question is marked as private.
  • FolderId; integer; Surrogate identifier of the folder where the question is stored.
  • FolderName; string; Name of the folder where the question is stored. Valid values are defined in the questionnaire folders list of values and include Active, Draft and Inactive.
  • InstructionsText; string; Instructions or additional information for the question.
  • LastUpdateDate; string; Date when the question was last updated.
  • LastUpdatedBy; string; Name of the user who last updated the question.
  • LatestVersion; string; Setting that indicates if this is the latest version of the question. This is a read-only attribute.
  • PrivacyFlag; boolean; Indicates whether the question is restricted for edits. A value of true indicates the question is private and the person specified as the owner can edit it.
  • PrivacyMeaning; string; Meaning of the privacy code. A value of Yes indicates the question is private and only the specified owner can edit. A value of No indicates the question isn't restricted for edits.
  • QuestionCode; string; Alphanumeric value that uniquely identifies a question defined for a subscriber.
  • QuestionId; integer; Surrogate identifier for the question.
  • QuestionText; string; Text of the question being asked.
  • QuestionType; string; Code identifying the type of question. Valid values are in the list commonLookupsLOV.
  • QuestionTypeMeaning; string; Meaning of the question type code.
  • RatingModelCode; string; Code identifying the rating model used to define the answer choices for the question.
  • RatingModelId; integer; Surrogate identifier of the rating model used to define the answer choices for the question.
  • RatingModelName; string; Name of the rating model used to define the answer choices for the question.
  • ResponseOrder; string; Code that shows whether the answer choices are presented in random sequence or in the defined sequence. Valid values include R to randomize the answer choices defined and V (vertical) to present the answer choices in the defined order.
  • ResponseOrderMeaning; string; Meaning of the response order code.
  • ResponseTypeId; integer; Surrogate identifier of the response type for the question.
  • ScoredFlag; boolean; Indicates the question is used in the score calculation.
  • Status; string; Status of the question. Valid values are A for Active, I for Inactive and D for Draft.
  • StatusMeaning; string; Meaning of the status code.
  • SubscriberCode; string; Code for the subscriber using the question. The list questionnaireSubscribersLOV includes the valid values.
  • SubscriberId; integer; Surrogate identifier for the subscriber.
  • SubscriberName; string; Name of the subscriber that the question is used for. Valid values are defined in the list questionnaireSubscribersLOV.
  • VersionNumber; integer; Version number of the question. New questions must have a version number of 1.

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description: This parameter orders a resource collection based on the specified fields.  The parameter value is a comma-separated string of attribute names, each optionally followed by a colon and "asc" or "desc".  Specify "asc" for ascending and "desc" for descending. The default value is "asc".  For example, ?orderBy=field1:asc,field2:desc

parameters.name: finder
parameters.description:
Used as a predefined finder to search the collection.

Format ?finder=<finderName>;<variableName>=<variableValue>,<variableName2>=<variableValue2>

The following are the available finder names and corresponding finder variables

  • PrimaryKey Finds the question that matches the primary key criteria specified.
    Finder Variables
    • QuestionId; integer; Surrogate identifier for the question.
    • VersionNumber; integer; Version number of the question. New questions must have version number 1.

x-adf-finder-formats.name: PrimaryKey
x-adf-finder-formats.description: Finds the question that matches the primary key criteria specified.

VersionNumber.description: Version number of the question. New questions must have version number 1.
VersionNumber.title: Version Number

parameters.name: effectiveDate
parameters.description: This query parameter is used to fetch resources which are effective dated as of the specified start date. The value of this query parameter is a string having a date in yyyy-MM-dd format

parameters.name: REST-Framework-Version
parameters.description: The protocol version between a REST client and service. If the client does not specify this header in the request the server will pick a default version for the API.

parameters.name: Metadata-Context
parameters.description: If the REST API supports runtime customizations, the shape of the service may change during runtime. The REST client may isolate itself from these changes or choose to interact with the latest version of the API by specifying this header. For example: Metadata-Context:sandbox="TrackEmployeeFeature".

parameters.name: Effective-Of
parameters.description: This header accepts a string value. The string is a semi-colon separated list of <parameter-name>=<parameter-value>. It is used to perform effective date range operations. The accepted parameters are RangeMode, RangeSpan, RangeStartDate, RangeEndDate, RangeStartSequence and RangeEndSequence. The parameter values are always strings. The possible values for RangeMode are SET_LOGICAL_START, SET_LOGICAL_END, END_DATE, SET_EFFECTIVE_START, SET_EFFECTIVE_END, REPLACE_CORRECTION, REPLACE_UPDATE, RECONCILE_CORRECTION, CORRECTION, RECONCILE_UPDATE, UPDATE, ZAP and DELETE_CHANGES. The possible values for RangeSpan are PHYSICAL_ROW_END_DATE and LOGICAL_ROW_END_DATE. The values for RangeStartDate and RangeEndDate have to be a string representation of a date in yyyy-MM-dd format. The value for RangeStartSequence and RangeEndSequence must be strings such that when parsed they yield positive integers.

default.description: The following table describes the default response for this task.

parameters.name: Upsert-Mode
parameters.description: Contains one of the following values: true or false. If true, the server performs an Upsert operation instead of a Create operation. During an Upsert operation, the server attempts to find an existing resource that matches the payload. If a match is found, the server updates the existing resource instead of creating a new one. If not found or false (default), the server performs a Create operation. Note that the Upsert operation isn't supported for date-effective REST resources.

parameters.name: REST-Framework-Version
parameters.description: The protocol version between a REST client and service. If the client does not specify this header in the request the server will pick a default version for the API.

AdhocFlag.title: Casual Question
AdhocFlag.description: Indicates whether the question is an ad hoc question. You can't create, modify, or delete ad hoc questions as these are casual questions and aren't maintained as setup data. The default value is false.

AllJobFamiliesFlag.title: All Job Families
AllJobFamiliesFlag.description: Indicates whether the question is applicable to all job families. This flag is only used for the questions defined for the recruiting subscriber.

AllJobFunctionsFlag.title: All Job Functions
AllJobFunctionsFlag.description: Indicates whether the question is applicable to all job functions. This flag is only used for the questions defined for the recruiting subscriber.

AllLocationsFlag.title: All Locations
AllLocationsFlag.description: Indicates whether the question is applicable to all locations. This flag is only used for the questions defined for the recruiting subscriber.

AllOrganizationsFlag.title: All Organizations
AllOrganizationsFlag.description: Indicates whether the question is applicable to all organizations. This flag is only used for the questions defined for the recruiting subscriber.

AllowAdditionalCommentsFlag.title: Allow Additional Comments
AllowAdditionalCommentsFlag.description: Indicates whether a participant can provide additional comments while responding to a question. Valid values are Y and N. The default value is N.

AllowAttachmentsFlag.title: Allow Attachments
AllowAttachmentsFlag.description: Indicates whether an attachment is allowed as a response to the question.

CandidateCode.title: Candidate Code
CandidateCode.description: Code identifying the candidate type to whom the question applies to. Only the recruiting subscriber uses this question.

CandidateCodeMeaning.title: Candidate Type
CandidateCodeMeaning.description: Meaning of the candidate code. Valid values are Internal, External and Both.

ClassificationCode.title: Classification Code
ClassificationCode.description: Code classifying the use of the question defined for Recruiting subscribers, like prescreening, and disqualification. Valid values are in the common lookups list of values.

ConditionalDisplayFlag.title: Conditional Display
ConditionalDisplayFlag.description: Indicates whether a question is conditionally displayed. When this is set to Y, controlling question code and controlling response are mandatory.

ControllingAnswerCode.title: Controlling Response
ControllingAnswerCode.description: Answer to the controlling question that determines whether the application displays the question or not.

ControllingAnswerId.title: Controlling Answer Id
ControllingAnswerId.description: Surrogate identifier of the controlling answer. Required if the conditional display is set to Y.

ControllingQuestionCode.title: Controlling Question Code
ControllingQuestionCode.description: Question code for the controlling question, response to which determines whether the application displays the question or not.

ControllingQuestionId.title: Controlling Question Id
ControllingQuestionId.description: Surrogate identifier of the controlling question. Required if the conditional display is set to Y.

CreatedBy.title: Created By
CreatedBy.description: Name of the user who created the question.

CreationDate.title: Creation Date
CreationDate.description: Date when the question was created.

Editor.title: Owner
Editor.description: Person identifier of the person with the privileges to edit the question, if the question is marked as private. Required if question is marked as private.

EditorPersonName.title: Person Name
EditorPersonName.description: Name of the person with privileges to edit the question marked as private. Required if question is marked as private.

EditorPersonNumber.title: Person Number
EditorPersonNumber.description: Person number of the person with privileges to edit the question marked private. Required if question is marked as private.

FolderId.title: Folder Id
FolderId.description: Surrogate identifier of the folder where the question is stored.

FolderName.title: Folder
FolderName.description: Name of the folder where the question is stored. Valid values are defined in the questionnaire folders list of values and include Active, Draft and Inactive.

InstructionsText.title: Instruction Text
InstructionsText.description: Instructions or additional information for the question.

LastUpdateDate.title: Last Update Date
LastUpdateDate.description: Date when the question was last updated.

LastUpdatedBy.title: Last Updated By
LastUpdatedBy.description: Name of the user who last updated the question.

LatestVersion.title: Latest Version
LatestVersion.description: Setting that indicates if this is the latest version of the question. This is a read-only attribute.

MaximumDate.title: Maximum Value
MaximumDate.description: Latest date to be supported as the response when a participant responds to a question.

MaximumPossibleScore.title: Maximum Possible Score
MaximumPossibleScore.description: Sum of the positive scores of possible answer choices for the question. For multiple choice questions, it's the sum of positive scores of the maximum number of selections allowed by the question. If the answer choices have no positive score defined, then the sum is zero.

MaximumResponses.title: Maximum Number of Selections
MaximumResponses.description: Maximum number of answer choices that a participant can select when responding to a multiple choice question.

MaximumThresholdScore.title: Maximum Calculated Score
MaximumThresholdScore.description: Maximum score allowed for the question when answered.

MinimumDate.title: Minimum Value
MinimumDate.description: Earliest date supported as the response when a participant responds to a question.

MinimumResponses.title: Minimum Number of Selections
MinimumResponses.description: Minimum number of answer choices that a participant can select when responding to a multiple choice question.

MinimumThresholdScore.title: Minimum Calculated Score
MinimumThresholdScore.description: Minimum score allowed for the question when answered.

OverrideDescriptionFlag.title: Override Rating Level Description
OverrideDescriptionFlag.description: Indicates whether the answer defined in a question came from a rating model level and if the description of the rating level was changed in the question.

PrivacyFlag.title: Privacy Code
PrivacyFlag.description: Indicates whether the question is restricted for edits. A value of true indicates the question is private and the person specified as the owner can edit it.

PrivacyMeaning.title: Privacy
PrivacyMeaning.description: Meaning of the privacy code. A value of Yes indicates the question is private and only the specified owner can edit. A value of No indicates the question isn't restricted for edits.

QuestionCode.title: Question Code
QuestionCode.description: Alphanumeric value that uniquely identifies a question defined for a subscriber.

QuestionText.title: Question Text
QuestionText.description: Text of the question being asked.

QuestionType.title: Question Type Code
QuestionType.description: Code identifying the type of question. Valid values are in the list commonLookupsLOV.

QuestionTypeMeaning.title: Question Type
QuestionTypeMeaning.description: Meaning of the question type code.

RatingModelCode.title: Rating Model Code
RatingModelCode.description: Code identifying the rating model used to define the answer choices for the question.

RatingModelId.title: Rating Model Id
RatingModelId.description: Surrogate identifier of the rating model used to define the answer choices for the question.

RatingModelName.title: Rating Model
RatingModelName.description: Name of the rating model used to define the answer choices for the question.

ResponseMaximumLength.title: Response Maximum Length
ResponseMaximumLength.description: Maximum allowed length of responses for a question of text response type.

ResponseMinimumLength.title: Response Minimum Length
ResponseMinimumLength.description: Minimum expected length of responses for a question of text response type.

ResponseOrder.title: Response Order Code
ResponseOrder.description: Code that shows whether the answer choices are presented in random sequence or in the defined sequence. Valid values include R to randomize the answer choices defined and V (vertical) to present the answer choices in the defined order.

ResponseOrderMeaning.title: Response Order
ResponseOrderMeaning.description: Meaning of the response order code.

ResponseType.title: Response Type Code
ResponseType.description: Response type allowed for the question. The list questionnaireResponseTypesLOV includes the valid values.

ResponseTypeId.title: Response Type Id
ResponseTypeId.description: Surrogate identifier of the response type for the question.

ResponseTypeMeaning.title: Response Type
ResponseTypeMeaning.description: Meaning of the response type code.

SampleSize.title: Sample Size
SampleSize.description: Maximum number of answer choices presented to the participant when responding to the question.

ScoredFlag.title: Score Question
ScoredFlag.description: Indicates the question is used in the score calculation.

Status.title: Status Code
Status.description: Status of the question. Valid values are A for Active, I for Inactive and D for Draft.

SubscriberCode.title: Subscriber Code
SubscriberCode.description: Code for the subscriber using the question. The list questionnaireSubscribersLOV includes the valid values.

SubscriberName.title: Subscriber Name
SubscriberName.description: Name of the subscriber that the question is used for. Valid values are defined in the list questionnaireSubscribersLOV.

UpdateAllowedFlag.title: Update Allowed
UpdateAllowedFlag.description: Indicates whether updates are allowed to this question.

VersionDescription.title: Version Description
VersionDescription.description: Description provided by the user when a new version of the question is created.

VersionNumber.title: Version Number
VersionNumber.description: Version number of the question. New questions must have a version number of 1.

answers.title: Answers
answers.description: The answers resource is a child of the questions resource. It provides a list of choices that a user can select from when answering single or multiple choice questions.

attachments.title: Attachments
attachments.description: The attachments resource is a child of the questions resource. It includes details of the attachments in a question.

jobFamilyContexts.title: Job Family Contexts
jobFamilyContexts.description: The jobFamilyContexts resource is a child of the questions resource. It provides the job family that the question applies to. This context is used only by the recruiting subscriber.

jobFunctionContexts.title: Job Function Contexts
jobFunctionContexts.description: The jobFunctionContexts resource is a child of the questions resource. It provides the job function that the question applies to. This context is used only by the recruiting subscriber.

links.title: Links
links.description: The link relations associated with the resource instance.

locationContexts.title: Location Contexts
locationContexts.description: The locationContexts resource is a child of the questions resource. It provides the recruiting location that the question applies to. This context is used only by the recruiting subscriber.

organizationContexts.title: Organization Contexts
organizationContexts.description: The organizationContexts resource is a child of the questions resource. It provides the recruiting organization that the question applies to. This context is used only by the recruiting subscriber.

AddlQuestionnaireId.title: Additional Questionnaire
AddlQuestionnaireId.description: Surrogate identifier of the questionnaire sent to candidates from whom additional information is requested. Used only for the recruiting subscriber.

AddlQuestionnaireVersionNumber.title: Additional Questionnaire
AddlQuestionnaireVersionNumber.description: Version number of the additional questionnaire sent to the participant. Used only for the recruiting subscriber.

AnswerCode.title: Answer Code
AnswerCode.description: Alphanumeric value that uniquely identifies the answer choice for a subscriber.

CorrectFlag.title: Correct Answer Code
CorrectFlag.description: Indicates whether the answer choice is correct or incorrect for a question.

CreatedBy.title: Created By
CreatedBy.description: Name of the user who created the answer choice.

CreationDate.title: Creation Date
CreationDate.description: Date when the answer choice was created.

LastUpdateDate.title: Last Update Date
LastUpdateDate.description: Date when the answer choice was last updated.

LastUpdatedBy.title: Last Updated By
LastUpdatedBy.description: Name of the user who last updated the answer choice.

LongAnswerText.title: Short Description
LongAnswerText.description: Answer text presented to the participant as a choice for a question when responding to a questionnaire.

QuestionAnswerId.title: Question Answer Id
QuestionAnswerId.description: Surrogate identifier of the answer choice for a question.

RatingLevelId.title: Rating Level Id
RatingLevelId.description: Surrogate identifier of the rating level from which the answer choice is created.

RatingLevelName.title: Rating Level
RatingLevelName.description: Rating level from which the answer was created.

ResponseFeedback.title: Response Feedback
ResponseFeedback.description: Feedback provided to the participant when the answer choice is chosen.

SequenceNumber.title: Sequence Number
SequenceNumber.description: Sequence in which the answer choice is presented during response by a participant.

ShortAnswerText.title: Short Text
ShortAnswerText.description: Short text of the answer choice.

attachments.title: Attachments
attachments.description: The attachments resource is a child of the questions resource. It includes details of the attachments in a question.

links.title: Links
links.description: The link relations associated with the resource instance.

AsyncTrackerId.title: Asynchronous Upload Tracker Id
AsyncTrackerId.description: Attribute provided for the exclusive use by the Attachment UI components to assist in uploading files.

AttachedDocumentId.title: Attached Document Id
AttachedDocumentId.description: Unique identifier of the attached document.

CreatedByUserName.title: Created By User Name
CreatedByUserName.description: User name of the user who created the attachment.

CreationDate.title: Creation Date

DatatypeCode.description: Attachment type supported for the question. Only file attachment is supported.

DownloadInfo.title: DownloadInfo
DownloadInfo.description: JSON object represented as a string that contains information used to programmatically retrieve a file attachment.

ErrorStatusCode.title: Error Status Code
ErrorStatusCode.description: Error status code of the attachment.

ErrorStatusMessage.title: Error Status Message
ErrorStatusMessage.description: Error status message of the attachment.

FileWebImage.description: Base64 encoded image of the file displayed in .png format when the source is a convertible image.

LastUpdatedByUserName.description: User name of the person who last updated the attachment.

PostProcessingAction.title: PostProcessingAction
PostProcessingAction.description: The name of the action that can be performed after an attachment is uploaded.

UploadedFileContentType.title: Uploaded File Content Type

UserName.description: User name of the person who uploaded the attachment.

MaximumResponses.description: Maximum number of answer choices that a participant can select when responding to a multiple choice question.

MaximumThresholdScore.title: Maximum Calculated Score
MaximumThresholdScore.description: Maximum score allowed for the question when answered.

MinimumDate.title: Minimum Value
MinimumDate.description: Earliest date supported as the response when a participant responds to a question.

MinimumResponses.title: Minimum Number of Selections
MinimumResponses.description: Minimum number of answer choices that a participant can select when responding to a multiple choice question.

MinimumThresholdScore.title: Minimum Calculated Score
MinimumThresholdScore.description: Minimum score allowed for the question when answered.

OverrideDescriptionFlag.title: Override Rating Level Description
OverrideDescriptionFlag.description: Indicates whether the answer defined in a question came from a rating model level and if the description of the rating level was changed in the question.

PrivacyFlag.title: Privacy Code
PrivacyFlag.description: Indicates whether the question is restricted for edits. A value of true indicates the question is private and the person specified as the owner can edit it.

QuestionCode.title: Question Code
QuestionCode.description: Alphanumeric value that uniquely identifies a question defined for a subscriber.

QuestionText.title: Question Text
QuestionText.description: Text of the question being asked.

QuestionType.title: Question Type Code
QuestionType.description: Code identifying the type of question. Valid values are in the list commonLookupsLOV.

RatingModelCode.title: Rating Model Code
RatingModelCode.description: Code identifying the rating model used to define the answer choices for the question.

RatingModelId.title: Rating Model Id
RatingModelId.description: Surrogate identifier of the rating model used to define the answer choices for the question.

ResponseMaximumLength.title: Response Maximum Length
ResponseMaximumLength.description: Maximum allowed length of responses for a question of text response type.

ResponseMinimumLength.title: Response Minimum Length
ResponseMinimumLength.description: Minimum expected length of responses for a question of text response type.

ResponseOrder.title: Response Order Code
ResponseOrder.description: Code that shows whether the answer choices are presented in random sequence or in the defined sequence. Valid values include R to randomize the answer choices defined and V (vertical) to present the answer choices in the defined order.

ResponseType.title: Response Type Code
ResponseType.description: Response type allowed for the question. The list questionnaireResponseTypesLOV includes the valid values.

ResponseTypeId.title: Response Type Id
ResponseTypeId.description: Surrogate identifier of the response type for the question.

SampleSize.title: Sample Size
SampleSize.description: Maximum number of answer choices presented to the participant when responding to the question.

ScoredFlag.title: Score Question
ScoredFlag.description: Indicates the question is used in the score calculation.

Status.title: Status Code
Status.description: Status of the question. Valid values are A for Active, I for Inactive and D for Draft.

SubscriberCode.title: Subscriber Code
SubscriberCode.description: Code for the subscriber using the question. The list questionnaireSubscribersLOV includes the valid values.

SubscriberName.title: Subscriber Name
SubscriberName.description: Name of the subscriber that the question is used for. Valid values are defined in the list questionnaireSubscribersLOV.

VersionDescription.title: Version Description
VersionDescription.description: Description provided by the user when a new version of the question is created.

VersionNumber.title: Version Number
VersionNumber.description: Version number of the question. New questions must have a version number of 1.

answers.title: Answers
answers.description: The answers resource is a child of the questions resource. It provides a list of choices that a user can select from when answering single or multiple choice questions.

attachments.title: Attachments
attachments.description: The attachments resource is a child of the questions resource. It includes details of the attachments in a question.

jobFamilyContexts.title: Job Family Contexts
jobFamilyContexts.description: The jobFamilyContexts resource is a child of the questions resource. It provides the job family that the question applies to. This context is used only by the recruiting subscriber.

jobFunctionContexts.title: Job Function Contexts
jobFunctionContexts.description: The jobFunctionContexts resource is a child of the questions resource. It provides the job function that the question applies to. This context is used only by the recruiting subscriber.

locationContexts.title: Location Contexts
locationContexts.description: The locationContexts resource is a child of the questions resource. It provides the recruiting location that the question applies to. This context is used only by the recruiting subscriber.

organizationContexts.title: Organization Contexts
organizationContexts.description: The organizationContexts resource is a child of the questions resource. It provides the recruiting organization that the question applies to. This context is used only by the recruiting subscriber.

AddlQuestionnaireId.title: Additional Questionnaire
AddlQuestionnaireId.description: Surrogate identifier of the questionnaire sent to candidates from whom additional information is requested. Used only for the recruiting subscriber.

AddlQuestionnaireVersionNumber.title: Additional Questionnaire
AddlQuestionnaireVersionNumber.description: Version number of the additional questionnaire sent to the participant. Used only for the recruiting subscriber.

AnswerCode.title: Answer Code
AnswerCode.description: Alphanumeric value that uniquely identifies the answer choice for a subscriber.

CorrectFlag.title: Correct Answer Code
CorrectFlag.description: Indicates whether the answer choice is correct or incorrect for a question.

LongAnswerText.title: Short Description
LongAnswerText.description: Answer text presented to the participant as a choice for a question when responding to a questionnaire.

RatingLevelId.title: Rating Level Id
RatingLevelId.description: Surrogate identifier of the rating level from which the answer choice is created.

ResponseFeedback.title: Response Feedback
ResponseFeedback.description: Feedback provided to the participant when the answer choice is chosen.

SequenceNumber.title: Sequence Number
SequenceNumber.description: Sequence in which the answer choice is presented during response by a participant.

ShortAnswerText.title: Short Text
ShortAnswerText.description: Short text of the answer choice.

attachments.title: Attachments
attachments.description: The attachments resource is a child of the questions resource. It includes details of the attachments in a question.

AsyncTrackerId.title: Asynchronous Upload Tracker Id
AsyncTrackerId.description: Attribute provided for the exclusive use by the Attachment UI components to assist in uploading files.

AttachedDocumentId.title: Attached Document Id
AttachedDocumentId.description: Unique identifier of the attached document.

DatatypeCode.title: Type
DatatypeCode.description: Attachment type supported for the question. Only file attachment is supported.

DmDocumentId.description: Surrogate identifier of the document attached to the questionnaire template.

DownloadInfo.title: DownloadInfo
DownloadInfo.description: JSON object represented as a string that contains information used to programmatically retrieve a file attachment.

ErrorStatusCode.title: Error Status Code
ErrorStatusCode.description: Error status code of the attachment.

ErrorStatusMessage.title: Error Status Message
ErrorStatusMessage.description: Error status message of the attachment.

PostProcessingAction.title: PostProcessingAction
PostProcessingAction.description: The name of the action that can be performed after an attachment is uploaded.

UploadedFileContentType.title: Uploaded File Content Type
UploadedFileContentType.description: Content type of the uploaded file.

Url.title: Url
Url.description: URL of a web page type attachment.

AsyncTrackerId.title: Asynchronous Upload Tracker Id
AsyncTrackerId.description: Attribute provided for the exclusive use by the Attachment UI components to assist in uploading files.

AttachedDocumentId.title: Attached Document Id
AttachedDocumentId.description: Unique identifier of the attached document.

DatatypeCode.title: Type
DatatypeCode.description: Attachment type supported for the question. Only file attachment is supported.

DmDocumentId.description: Surrogate identifier of the document attached to the questionnaire template.

DownloadInfo.title: DownloadInfo
DownloadInfo.description: JSON object represented as a string that contains information used to programmatically retrieve a file attachment.

ErrorStatusCode.title: Error Status Code
ErrorStatusCode.description: Error status code of the attachment.

ErrorStatusMessage.title: Error Status Message
ErrorStatusMessage.description: Error status message of the attachment.

ExpirationDate.title: Expiration Date
ExpirationDate.description: Expiration date of the attachment. This is a read-only attribute.

PostProcessingAction.title: PostProcessingAction
PostProcessingAction.description: The name of the action that can be performed after an attachment is uploaded.

UploadedFileContentType.title: Uploaded File Content Type
UploadedFileContentType.description: Content type of the uploaded file.

Url.title: Url
Url.description: URL of a web page type attachment.

JobFamilyId.title: Job Family Id
JobFamilyId.description: Surrogate identifier of the job family defined as a context to the question.

JobFamilyName.title: Job Family Name
JobFamilyName.description: Name of the job family context defined for the question.

JobFunctionCode.title: Job Function Code
JobFunctionCode.description: Code of the job function context defined for the question.

LocationId.title: Location Id
LocationId.description: Surrogate identifier of the recruiting location defined as a context to the question.

LocationName.title: Location Name
LocationName.description: Name of the recruiting location context defined for the question.

OrganizationId.title: Organization Id
OrganizationId.description: Surrogate identifier of the recruiting organization defined as a context for the question.

OrganizationName.title: Organization Name
OrganizationName.description: Name of the recruiting organization defined as a context for the question.

Metadata-Context.description: If the REST API supports runtime customizations, the shape of the service may change during runtime. The REST client may isolate itself from these changes or choose to interact with the latest version of the API by specifying this header. For example: Metadata-Context:sandbox="TrackEmployeeFeature".

REST-Framework-Version.description: The protocol version between a REST client and service. If the client does not specify this header in the request the server will pick a default version for the API.

questions-item-patch-request.description: The following table describes the body parameters in the request for this task.

questions-item-post-request.description: The following table describes the body parameters in the request for this task.
